description: Triangle Assessment Set. v1.5: prompts and vertex labels shortened for cognitive load reduction. Prompt carries full scenario; each vertex is one short idea under 10 words. Measured dimensions, dimensionIds, framing, and coverage map unchanged from v1.3.
scoring_note: All triangles use standard drag-toward mechanics. For gain-framed triangles, proximity to a vertex indicates affinity with that dimension. For loss-framed triangles, proximity to a vertex indicates that the absence of that dimension is most painful - which maps to a high score on that dimension. Formula: score = (barycentric_coordinate × 4) + 1, mapping [0,1] to [1,5]. Aggregate multiple measurements per dimension by simple average.

triangles.title: The Meaning of a Win
triangles.prompt: You just nailed something - an exam, a competition, a personal goal. Which feeling hits hardest?

b.label: You did something most people couldn't

triangles.tension_note: Three distinct sources of post-achievement satisfaction: impact on others, internal depth, and external recognition.

triangles.title: Pick Your Path
triangles.prompt: Three opportunities - each great in only one way, each with a real cost. Which do you actually choose?

a.label: Great money, but stressful and relentless

c.label: People you love, but long hours

triangles.tension_note: Classic trade-off between money, time, and community. Each option has a real cost, ensuring the placement reveals genuine priority.

triangles.title: Your Ideal Day
triangles.prompt: If you could design how you spend your time every day, which matters most?

b.label: Total clarity - you always know what's coming

c.label: Pure creativity - you build something original

triangles.tension_note: Three environmental needs that genuinely compete: autonomy, security, and creativity.

triangles.title: When Everything Keeps Changing
triangles.prompt: You're working toward something important but the rules and goals keep shifting. What actually keeps you going?

a.label: Cracking the problem that stops everyone else

b.label: Staying calm when you don't have all the answers

c.label: Picking up new skills faster than the situation changes

triangles.tension_note: Three distinct responses to sustained difficulty: the achiever, the stabiliser, and the fast adapter.

triangles.title: The Energy Drain
triangles.prompt: Which of these would burn you out the fastest?

b.label: Zero impact - your work changes nothing

triangles.tension_note: Loss-framed. Proximity to a vertex means that absence is the most painful.

triangles.title: The Dealbreaker
triangles.prompt: You have a great opportunity in front of you. One thing is missing. Which missing thing makes you walk away?

b.label: No freedom - every idea needs approval

triangles.tension_note: Loss-framed. Three dealbreakers: growth, freedom to experiment, and collaboration.

triangles.title: The Future Self
triangles.prompt: Ten years from now, you made it. What is the core of that success?

a.label: Everyone in your field knows your name

b.label: You went deeper into your craft than almost anyone

c.label: You built the team everyone else credits

triangles.tension_note: Three archetypes of success: reputation, depth, and team impact.

triangles.title: What You Protect
triangles.prompt: High-stakes moment - a competition, a performance, something that really matters. You can only walk away with one thing. Which is it?

a.label: Knowing you hit the edge of your ability

b.label: Being seen as the best in the room

c.label: Building something that keeps paying off

triangles.tension_note: Loss framing forces honest choice between internal achievement, external reputation, and stability.

triangles.title: How You Work Best
triangles.prompt: Big creative project, powerful tools available to help. Where do you find your flow?

a.label: Running the whole system toward a big goal

b.label: Making the thing yourself - hands on

c.label: Running fast experiments to find what works

triangles.tension_note: Three modes: orchestration, personal creation, and rapid iteration.

triangles.title: When the Plan Falls Apart
triangles.prompt: Something you worked on for months suddenly falls through. What do you do first?

a.label: Already looking for the next thing

b.label: Turning the mess into a new plan

c.label: Checking that the people around you are okay

triangles.tension_note: Loss-framed. Three responses to disruption: the adapter, the stabiliser, and the social glue.

triangles.title: The Hidden Catch
triangles.prompt: You got it - great opportunity, interesting work, real rewards. But one thing is secretly wrong. Which would eventually break you?

a.label: No say - you just run someone else's system

b.label: Your effort is invisible - credit always goes to the group

c.label: Unpredictable rewards - great sometimes, anxious others

triangles.tension_note: Loss framing surfaces background needs: autonomy, individual visibility, and financial predictability.

answer_interpretations.balanced: All three feel somewhat relevant; no strong pull in any direction. Your profile combines all triangles; this question is one input.
answer_interpretations.dominant_a: Strong pull toward autonomy: lacking say or control would eventually break you.
answer_interpretations.dominant_b: Strong pull toward visibility: invisible effort or credit going to the group would eventually break you.
answer_interpretations.dominant_c: Strong pull toward predictability: unpredictable rewards would eventually break you.
